seo.title: Chon Buri Province, Thailand — Complete Guide 2026
seo.metaDescription: Everything about Chon Buri Province: Pattaya, Koh Larn, Sanctuary of Truth, and Si Racha — birthplace of Sriracha sauce. Area: 4,363 km², population: 1.6 million. Plan your visit.

overview: Chon Buri is a coastal province in eastern Thailand fronting the Gulf of Thailand, best known as home to Pattaya — one of Southeast Asia's most visited beach resort cities. Beyond the neon of Pattaya, the province offers a surprising range of experiences: pristine island beaches on Koh Larn and Koh Si Chang, the quiet charm of the fishing town Si Racha (birthplace of the world-famous Sriracha sauce), the family-friendly Bang Saen beach, and the vast Khao Kheow Open Zoo. The name Chon Buri derives from Sanskrit meaning 'City of Water,' and water defines the province — from its 160-km coastline to the seafood markets of Ang Sila and the ferry-linked islands offshore.
geography: Chon Buri covers 4,363 km² along the eastern shore of the Gulf of Thailand, about 80 km southeast of Bangkok. The western portion is a coastal plain with a 160-km shoreline, including the beaches of Bang Saen, Pattaya, and Jomtien. The eastern interior rises gently into forested hills, home to Khao Kheow Open Zoo and several reservoirs. Offshore islands include Koh Larn (Coral Island), Koh Si Chang, and the Samae San island group. The province sits at the western edge of Thailand's Eastern Seaboard industrial zone, making it both a tourism hub and an economic powerhouse.
highlights: Pattaya — Thailand's most famous beach resort city with Walking Street, Jomtien Beach, and hundreds of hotels
Koh Larn (Coral Island) — crystal-clear water and white sand beaches just 45 minutes by ferry from Pattaya
Sanctuary of Truth — a 105-meter-tall all-wood temple covered in intricate carvings, Pattaya's most impressive landmark
Si Racha — the quiet coastal town where Sriracha sauce was invented in 1932, with excellent seafood restaurants
Khao Kheow Open Zoo — a 2,000-acre open zoo with over 300 species in natural habitats, great for families
Nong Nooch Tropical Garden — a 500-acre botanical garden with Thai cultural shows and an orchid collection
Bang Saen Beach — a popular local beach getaway with affordable seafood, just 100 km from Bangkok
Ang Sila — a centuries-old fishing village famous for its stone mortar and pestle workshops and fresh seafood market

bestTimeToVisit.peak: Nov–Feb: Cool season with temperatures of 25–31°C, low humidity, and minimal rain. Best for beach activities, island hopping, and outdoor sightseeing. Peak tourist season with highest hotel prices in Pattaya.
bestTimeToVisit.shoulder: Mar–May: Hot season with temperatures reaching 33–36°C. The sea is calm and warm, ideal for snorkeling around Koh Larn. Songkran (April) brings massive water fights to Pattaya's beach road.
bestTimeToVisit.lowSeason: Jun–Oct: Monsoon season with regular afternoon downpours and occasional storms. Sea conditions can be rough, and some ferries to islands may be cancelled. Hotel prices in Pattaya drop 30–50%.
bestTimeToVisit.recommendation: Visit November to February for the best combination of cool weather and calm seas. March and April are great for water activities if you can handle the heat. Avoid Pattaya during major holiday weekends (Thai New Year, Chinese New Year) when traffic from Bangkok is extreme.

topAttractions.name: Sanctuary of Truth (Prasat Sajja Tham)
topAttractions.description: A 105-meter-tall temple made entirely of hand-carved wood without a single nail, blending Thai, Khmer, Chinese, and Indian artistic styles. Still under construction after more than 40 years, it stands dramatically on the Pattaya coastline. Guided tours and cultural shows included.
topAttractions.entranceFee: 500 THB (~$14 USD) adults, 250 THB children. Evening tour: 700 THB

topAttractions.name: Koh Larn (Coral Island)
topAttractions.description: A small island 7.5 km off the Pattaya coast with six white-sand beaches and crystal-clear turquoise water. Tawaen Beach is the busiest; Samae Beach and Tien Beach are quieter. Rent a motorbike to explore the island or take a glass-bottom boat for snorkeling.
topAttractions.entranceFee: Ferry: 30 THB each way from Bali Hai Pier (public ferry). Speedboat: 200–300 THB/person

topAttractions.name: Nong Nooch Tropical Garden
topAttractions.description: A massive 500-acre botanical garden and cultural village featuring themed gardens (French, European, Stonehenge replica), a large orchid nursery, elephant shows, and traditional Thai cultural performances. One of Pattaya's most popular family attractions.
topAttractions.entranceFee: 500 THB (~$14 USD) adults including cultural show. Online discounts often available from 220 THB

topAttractions.name: Khao Kheow Open Zoo
topAttractions.description: A 2,000-acre open-concept zoo in Si Racha district housing over 300 animal species in naturalistic habitats. Highlights include the night safari, giraffe feeding, and a walk-through aviary. One of the best zoos in Thailand and a great family day trip from Pattaya or Bangkok.
topAttractions.entranceFee: 300 THB (~$8.50 USD) adults, 150 THB children. Night safari: 350 THB extra

topAttractions.name: Tiger Topia Sriracha Zoo
topAttractions.description: Formerly the Sriracha Tiger Zoo, this wildlife park in Si Racha is home to over 200 Bengal tigers, crocodiles, and performing animals. Watch tiger shows, crocodile feeding, and interact with tiger cubs. A Chon Buri institution since 1997.

topAttractions.name: Koh Si Chang
topAttractions.description: A historically significant island in the upper Gulf of Thailand, once a royal summer retreat. Visit the hilltop Chinese temple (San Chao Pho Khao Yai), the ruins of Rama V's summer palace, and quiet beaches with far fewer tourists than Koh Larn. Ferries from Si Racha pier take 40 minutes.
topAttractions.entranceFee: Ferry: 50 THB each way from Si Racha. Island entry: free

topAttractions.name: Bang Saen Beach
topAttractions.description: A popular local beach just 100 km from Bangkok, favored by Thai families for its affordable seafood restaurants and relaxed vibe. Less touristy than Pattaya, with a long promenade, night market, and the Ang Sila fishing village nearby. The nearby Institute of Marine Science has a small aquarium.

localFood: Sriracha Sauce — Si Racha is the birthplace of the original Sriraja Panich chili sauce, created by Thanom Chakkapak in 1932 as a dipping sauce for fresh seafood
Hoi Kraeng Luak (Blanched Cockles) — Ang Sila and Bang Saen are famous for fresh cockles served with spicy seafood sauce, a local staple
Pla Kraphong Neung Manao — steamed sea bass with lime, garlic, and chili; the coastal province's signature fish dish using catch-of-the-day
Khao Lam — sticky rice with coconut cream and black beans steamed in bamboo, sold at roadside stalls throughout the province
Kung Mangkon Phao (Grilled Lobster) — fresh grilled lobster at Si Racha and Ang Sila seafood restaurants, cheaper than Bangkok prices
Pu Phat Phong Kari (Crab Curry) — stir-fried crab in yellow curry and egg, best enjoyed at seafood restaurants along the coast
Khanom Jeen Nam Ya — rice noodles with spicy fish curry sauce, a popular breakfast in Chon Buri's local markets
Mango Sticky Rice with Durian — seasonal variation of the Thai classic, using Chon Buri-grown durian from the eastern orchards
culture: Chon Buri has a rich cultural identity shaped by its coastal heritage and diverse communities. The province is famous for the annual Buffalo Racing Festival held in October in Mueang Chon Buri — a tradition over 100 years old where decorated water buffalo race down the main street. Si Racha hosts a large Thai-Chinese community that celebrates Chinese New Year with dragon boat races. Pattaya's international character makes it one of the most multicultural cities in Thailand, with influences from around the world visible in its restaurants, temples, and festivals. The Chon Buri coast has been a fishing community for centuries, and the traditional craft of carving stone mortars and pestles from local granite persists in Ang Sila village.
gettingThere: Chon Buri is one of the most accessible provinces in Thailand, just 80 km southeast of Bangkok. By car: take Motorway 7 (Bangkok–Pattaya) — the drive takes about 1.5 hours. By bus: frequent air-conditioned buses from Bangkok's Eastern Bus Terminal (Ekkamai) and Mo Chit to Pattaya (2–2.5 hours, 120–150 THB). By train: the Eastern Line runs from Bangkok (Hua Lamphong) to Chon Buri town and Pattaya, though service is slow and infrequent. The nearest airport is U-Tapao (UTP) near Sattahip, served by some domestic and international flights. Bangkok's Suvarnabhumi Airport (BKK) is about 90 minutes from Pattaya by taxi or shuttle.
gettingAround: Within Pattaya, songthaews (baht buses) run fixed routes along Beach Road and Second Road for 10 THB per ride. Motorbike taxis are available on every corner (20–100 THB depending on distance). Grab ride-hailing works in Pattaya and Si Racha. Renting a motorbike (200–300 THB/day) or car (800–1,500 THB/day) is recommended for exploring beyond Pattaya to attractions like Khao Kheow Open Zoo and Nong Nooch Garden. For island hopping, public ferries depart from Bali Hai Pier (Koh Larn) and Si Racha pier (Koh Si Chang).
travelTips: Pattaya is two cities in one — the beach road side is touristy and loud, while Jomtien (south) and Naklua (north) are much quieter and more family-friendly
Take the 30-THB public ferry to Koh Larn instead of booking speedboat tours at 10x the price — you get the same beaches
Visit Ang Sila fish market early morning (6–8 AM) for the freshest and cheapest seafood — prices rise as the day goes on
Avoid driving the Bangkok–Pattaya motorway on Friday evenings and Sunday evenings — traffic from weekenders can add 2+ hours
The Sanctuary of Truth is best visited in late afternoon when the wooden carvings are lit by golden hour light
Si Racha makes an excellent half-day side trip from Pattaya — try the original Sriracha sauce at local seafood restaurants
Negotiate songthaew prices in Pattaya before boarding for non-standard routes — the flat 10 THB rate only applies to the fixed Beach Road route
U-Tapao airport south of Pattaya is expanding rapidly — check for direct flights before automatically booking via Bangkok
